webLinks.linkText: White Bay Power Station - Biennale of Sydney

properties.publicTransport: Route 441 and 442 buses run regularly to Rozelle. As well, route 443 heritage buses will run on weekends and public holidays. See details at transportnsw.info
properties.otherAdvice:
The Biennale of Sydney will be held at White Bay Power Station open from Tuesday-Sundays 10am-5pm daily, from Saturday 14 March to Sunday 14 June. The exhibition will also be open on public holidays.

Traffic will be heavy on roads approaching White Bay Power Station as crowds travel to and from the event,

Attendees driving should carpool with their group, plan to arrive early as local parking is limited.

Motorists should allow extra travel time on Victoria Rd, the City West Link and Robert St.

properties.publicTransport: Light rail and buses run regularly to Moore Park. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
The Sydney Family Show will be held at The Entertainment Quarter from Thursday 2 to Sunday 19 April. Show starts at 10am daily and will finish between 3pm and 5pm depending on the day.

  • Traffic is expected to be heavier than usual on Anzac Pde, Eastern Distributor, Moore Park Rd, Cleveland St and Lang Rd approaching Moore Park as families travel to and from the Show each day. Motorists should allow extra travel time in the area and be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads.
  • The precinct will be particularly busy while events are held at neighbouring Allianz Stadium, Sydney Cricket Ground and Hordern Pavilion so allow plenty of extra travel time as parking will be in demand on these days.
  • Driver Ave will be partially closed outside the stadiums at various times on game days on 17, 18 and 19 April.

Special event clearways will be in effect on roads in and around Moore Park on 17, 18 and 19 April (game days) while the Show takes place so check signs carefully before parking as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies.

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types, and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

arrangementElements.html:
ROAD CLOSURES

Driver Ave at Moore Park will be partially closed during events at nearby SCG and Allianz Stadium on the following dates: 

  • Friday 17 April from 3.30pm 
  • Saturday 18 April from 4.30pm 
  • Sunday 19 April from 11am

SPECIAL EVENT CLEARWAYS

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types, and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.


Special event clearways will be in place on roads in Moore Park for events at neighbouring SCG and Allianz Stadium on sections of Albion Street, Lang Road and Moore Park Road, Cleveland Street and Fitzroy Street.  

Clearways will be in effect on the following dates and times, check signs carefully to avoid being towed: 

  • Friday 17 April from 6am to midnight for the Super Rugby and AFL Derby
  • Saturday 18 April from 6am to 11pm for the A-League Sydney FC v Perth Glory
  • Sunday 19 April from 6am to 7pm for NRL Roosters v Knights

properties.publicTransport: Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
April shows at the TikTok Entertainment Centre:

  • Thursday 16 April: In Conversation with Pamela Anderson from 7pm to 9pm
  • Friday 17 April: Guo Degang & Yuqian Cross Talk Show from 7.30pm to 10.30pm
  • Sunday 19 April: ITZY from 7pm to 10pm
  • Thursday 30 April to Saturday 2 May: Guy Sebastian. Shows start at 7pm nightly.

Traffic may be heavier than usual as attendees travel to and from the event.

Motorists should allow extra travel time and be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads.

webLinks.linkText: NSW Government - Anzac Day in NSW

properties.publicTransport: Travel on public transport is free on Anzac Day for veterans and families. Additional early morning services will run. Buses will be diverting while roads are closed. Light rail services will not run between Town Hall and Circular Quay until 6.15am due to the Dawn Service, and may be temporarily held at stops or partially closed between 12.30pm and 1.30pm due to the March. Visit transportnsw.info for details.
properties.otherAdvice:
Major roads will be closed in the Sydney CBD for the Anzac Day Dawn Service in Martin Place from 4.20am and for the March on Elizabeth St from 9am.  

Motorists should avoid travelling through the CBD while roads are closed or expect delays and allow extra travel time. While roads are closed travel east/west across the CBD is maintained via Bridge St or via Goulburn St. 

Key road closures:  

  • From 12am to 8am: Macquarie St north of Albert St.  
  • From 1am to 3pm: Pitt St and Castlereagh St between Hunter St and King St. A westbound section of Hunter St between Elizabeth St and Hamilton St. Hosking Pl from Castlereagh St.  
  • From 1am to 6pm: Angel Pl and Ash St.  
  • From 6am to 3pm: Major roads between Bridge St in Circular Quay and Goulburn St in the CBD South, including sections of Bathurst St, Bent St, Bligh St, Castlereagh St, Elizabeth St, Gresham St, Hunter St, King St, Liverpool St, Loftus St, Market St, O’Connell St, Park St, Phillip St, Pitt St and St James Rd. 

There will be no managed vehicle access or access to/from driveways within the road closure footprint for the safety of participants and spectators. Pedestrians can cross the March route at the designated crossing point on Elizabeth St at Park St and Hunter St, and at underground crossings at Bathurst St, Museum Station and Martin Place Station. 

Cycleway closures will be in place on Pitt St from 1am to 3pm, and on sections of Castlereagh St, King St, Liverpool St, Park St, Pitt St and Phillip St from 6am to 3pm. 

Extensive special event clearway parking restrictions will be in effect on various major roads in the Sydney CBD with some starting from 10pm on Friday 24 April. Clearways will be in effect from 1am around Martin Place for the Dawn Service, and from 5.30am on all other major roads.   

All major road closures and clearways are due to be lifted by 3pm.  

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types and apply even to local residents, businesses or TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies so check signs carefully on the day and the night before as clearways come into effect in the early morning. If your vehicle is towed, you can search for your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types and apply even to local residents, businesses or TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies so check signs carefully on the day and the night before as clearways come into effect in the early morning. If your vehicle is towed, you can search for your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

properties.publicTransport: Buses may experience delays through Mascot while closures are in place. Visit transportnsw.info for service disruption information.
properties.otherAdvice:
Anzac Day commemorations in Mascot on Sunday 19 April. A march will travel along King St from 2.15pm, finishing at Mascot Memorial Park by 2.45pm.

For the safety of the marchers, NSW Police will implement rolling road closures along Botany Rd northbound lanes from King St to Coward St from 2pm until 3pm. Sections of King St, MacIntosh St, Forster St and Coward St will also close, and there will be no access to Botany Rd northbound during this time. 

Special event clearway parking restrictions will also be in place effect from 12.30pm to 4pm on Sunday 19 April at the following locations:

  • Botany Rd between King St and Coward St (western side/northbound)
  • Coward St between Botany Rd and Henry Kendall Crescent (both sides)
  • King St between Botany Rd and approx. 90m west of Botany Rd (both sides)

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types, and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

properties.publicTransport: Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
Road closures will be in place for an Anzac Day Dawn Service held outside the Brighton Le Sands RSL Club.

Bay St will be closed in both directions between The Grand Parade and Moate Ave from 5.15am to approx. 7am.

Motorists should follow the directions of traffic controllers on-site and detour around the closure via local roads.

properties.publicTransport: Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
NRL: Dragons v Sea Eagles at WIN Stadium. Kick-off at 6pm, full time around 8pm.

Traffic will be heavier than usual on roads approaching the Wollongong CBD and on local roads as fans travel to and from the match.

Local roads around the stadium may close before, during and after the show for the safety of pedestrians.

Fans driving to the game on the freeway are encouraged to use the Masters Rd exit to avoid congestion at Mount Ousley Rd, both in and out of Wollongong.

Motorists should allow extra time for parking and be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads.

properties.publicTransport: Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
NRL: Wests Tigers v Knights at Campbelltown Sports Stadium. Kick-off at 4.05pm, full time around 6.05pm.

Traffic may be heavier than usual as fans travel to the match in the already busy evening peak. Allow extra travel time on roads including Pembroke Rd, Rose Payten Dr and Leumeah Rd.

Motorists should also be aware of increased pedestrian activity in the area.

roads.mainStreet: Pembroke Road and Rose Payten Drive

properties.publicTransport: Match tickets include travel on transport. Buses will divert while roads are closed after the game. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
NRL: Eels v Titans at CommBank Stadium in Parramatta. NRL kick-off at 2pm, full time around 3.45pm.

With thousands of fan expected, traffic will be heavy on roads approaching Parramatta and nearby car parks are expected to be in high demand.

Allow extra travel time on roads including Parramatta Road, Church Street, Victoria Road, James Ruse Drive and Pennant Hills Road. 

Motorists should also exercise caution as pedestrian activity will be high on roads between Parramatta Interchange and the stadium. 

Drivers are reminded that light rail services are running through Parramatta CBD. Trams can’t stop quickly or easily – so exercise extra caution when driving in the area. 

Road closures 

Roads around the stadium will close from 3.45pm to 4.15pm approx. to allow crowds to safely leave the venue. Roads will re-open once crowds have cleared the area. 

  • O’Connell Street from Grose Street to George Street 
  • Ross Street from O’Connell Street to Trott Street 
  • Victoria Road from O’Connell Street to Villiers Street 

While closures are in place motorists are encouraged to avoid driving through Parramatta CBD and detour around the closures via Grose Street, Brickfield Street, Victoria Road and Wilde Avenue in both directions.

properties.publicTransport: Match tickets include travel on transport. Buses will divert while roads are closed after the game. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
A-League Men: Western Sydney Wanderers v Sydney FC at CommBank Stadium in Parramatta. Kick-off at 7.35pm, full time around 9.30pm.

With thousands of fans expected at the derby clash, traffic will be heavy on roads approaching Parramatta and nearby car parks are expected to be in high demand.

Allow extra travel time on roads including Parramatta Road, Church Street, Victoria Road, James Ruse Drive and Pennant Hills Road. 

Motorists should also exercise caution as pedestrian activity will be high on roads between Parramatta Interchange and the stadium. 

Drivers are reminded that light rail services are running through Parramatta CBD. Trams can’t stop quickly or easily – so exercise extra caution when driving in the area. 

Road closures 

Roads around the stadium will close from 9.30pm to 10pm approx. to allow crowds to safely leave the venue. Roads will re-open once crowds have cleared the area. 

  • O’Connell Street from Grose Street to George Street 
  • Ross Street from O’Connell Street to Trott Street 
  • Victoria Road from O’Connell Street to Villiers Street 

While closures are in place motorists are encouraged to avoid driving through Parramatta CBD and detour around the closures via Grose Street, Brickfield Street, Victoria Road and Wilde Avenue in both directions.

St Euphemia Bankstown Good Friday Easter Procession is taking place and road closures will be in place from 8:30pm to 11pm on sections of South Terrace, North Terrace, East Terrace, West Terrace and Raymond St.

Motorists are advised to take extra care travelling in the area and expect some delays.

properties.publicTransport: Route 306, 307 & 350 will be diverting during the closure period. Visit transportnsw.info for service disruption information.
properties.otherAdvice:
Road closures will be in place for St Catherine's Greek Orthodox Easter celebrations in Mascot.

Coward St will be closed in both directions between O Riordan St and Botany Rd from 7:45pm to 10pm.

Allow extra travel time in the area and follow the instructions of traffic controllers.

High volumes of holiday traffic are expected approaching and through Nowra, Jervis Bay, and Milton/Ulladulla during peak holiday travel periods.

For details about known holiday traffic pinch points, see the NSW Holiday and Journey planning website and the pinch point journey planning tool.

High volumes of holiday traffic are expected approaching Heatherbrae, Hexham and towards Beresfield during peak holiday travel periods.

For details about known holiday traffic pinch points, see the NSW Holiday and Journey planning website and the pinch point journey planning tool.

High volumes of holiday traffic are expected through Lithgow during peak holiday travel periods due to the closure of Great Western Highway at Victoria Pass.

For details about known holiday traffic pinch points, see the NSW Holiday and Journey planning website and the pinch point journey planning tool.

For more information about the work we are undertaking at Victoria Pass, click here.

High volumes of holiday traffic are expected from Wahroonga to Calga on the M1 Pacific Motorway during peak holiday travel periods.

For details about known holiday traffic pinch points, see the NSW Holiday and Journey planning website and the pinch point journey planning tool.

High volumes of holiday traffic are expected approaching the NSW-Queensland border during peak holiday travel periods. You can toggle to view traffic information supplied by QLD on Live Traffic NSW. For details about known holiday traffic pinch points, see the NSW Holiday and Journey planning website and the pinch point journey planning tool.

Road closures in place from 7am - 3pm:

  • Endeavour Drive - between Cliff Road and Endeavour Drive

properties.publicTransport: Route buses 343 and 358 will be diverting around Gardeners Rd while closures are in place. Check transportnsw.info bus alerts for more details.
properties.otherAdvice:
Roads around St Spyridon Church in Kingsford will be closed as large crowds are expected to attend Greek Easter celebrations and processions on Friday 10 and Saturday 11 April.

Road closures:

  • Gardeners Rd will be closed between Doncaster Ave and Bruce St from 8pm to 10:30pm on Friday 10 April and from 11pm to 1:30am on Saturday 11 to Sunday 12 April.

Motorists should follow detours, allow extra travel time and expect delays in the area while closures are in place.

properties.publicTransport: Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
Machine Gun Kelly performs at Qudos Bank Arena in Sydney Olympic Park on Tuesday 14 April. Show runs from 8pm

  • Traffic delays are expected as concertgoers make their way to Olympic Park during busy evening peaks.
  • Motorists should carpool and allow extra travel time on roads including the M4 Mwy, Homebush Bay Dr, Concord Rd, Lane Cove Rd, Parramatta Rd, Centenary Dr and Silverwater Rd.
  • If you have to drive, pre-book parking online in advance to secure a space, available until 11:59pm the night before the show unless sold out prior. Please note that P6 car park is closed for Easter Show infrastructure.
  • If you have pre-booked your parking, follow the directions of staff and have your parking ticket ready to show at the car park entry.
  • Use Silverwater Rd, Holker St and Hill Rd to access precinct car parks and avoid heavy traffic expected on Homebush Bay Drive and Underwood Rd. 

Road closures will be in place before, during and after the Easter Show for event set up and pack down. Check out the Sydney Olympic Park Authority interactive map for more details. For detailed closure and access changes in the precinct see the Olympic Park Community page.

arrangementElements.html:
Road closures and changes to parklands will be in place within the Sydney Olympic Park Precinct before, during and after the Easter Show for event set up and pack down. 

Check out the Sydney Olympic Park Authority interactive map for more details. For detailed closure and access changes in the precinct see the Olympic Park Community page. 

Monday 9 March at 1.30am to Tuesday 21 April at 1.30am:   

  • Olympic Boulevard 100m north of Dawn Fraser Avenue adjacent to Cathy Freeman Park amenities, to 200m south of Edwin Flack Avenue outside Qudos Bank Arena.  

Thursday 19 March at 1.30am to Tuesday 21 April at 1.30am:   

  • Australia Avenue from Murray Rose Avenue to Kevin Coombs Avenue. 
  • Showground Road from Murray Rose Avenue to Grand Parade. 
  • Grand Parade from Olympic Boulevard to Australia Avenue.

Autumn Carnival: Stakes Day at Royal Randwick Racecourse. First race at 11.25am, last race from 4.55pm.

Traffic may be heavier than usual on Anzac Pde, Doncaster Ave, High St, Alison Rd and Darley Rd as crowds travel to and from the event.

Motorists should allow extra travel time and be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads.

Special event clearways will be in place at the following times and locations: 

  • 4am – 7pm: Darley Road between Alison Road and the Grand Drive roundabout (western side) 
  • 8am – 7pm: Ascot Street between Doncaster Avenue and the roundabout at the entry to Randwick Racecourse (both sides) 
  • 3pm – 7pm: High Street between Gate Two Avenue and Anzac Parade (southern side)

Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

A-League Men: Sydney FC v Perth Glory at Allianz Stadium. Kick off from 7.35pm, full time at 9.20pm

Traffic will be heavier than usual in the area. Motorists should allow extra travel time on Anzac Pde, Lang Rd, Oxford St, Syd Einfeld Drive, Darley Rd, York Rd and Alison Rd.

  • Driver Avenue will be partially closed outside the stadium from 4.30pm and will reopen once crowds have safely cleared the area.  
  • No through-traffic will be available on Driver Avenue, however access to EP3 grass car park and drop-off areas maintained from Lang Road. Car Park open from 4.35pm.
  • Special event clearways will be in place on roads in Moore Park and surrounding areas from 3pm to 11pm on sections of Albion Street, Lang Road and Fitzroy Street and from 6am to 11pm on Cleveland Street.  

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator

properties.otherAdvice:
NRL: Wests Tigers v Broncos at Campbelltown Sports Stadium. Kick-off at 7.30pm, full time around 9.30pm.

Traffic may be heavier than usual as fans travel to the match. Allow extra travel time on roads including Pembroke Rd, Rose Payten Dr and Leumeah Rd.

Motorists should also be aware of increased pedestrian activity in the area.

roads.mainStreet: Pembroke Road and Rose Payten Drive

properties.publicTransport: Match tickets include travel on transport. Buses will divert while roads are closed after the game. Buses replace trains between Penrith, Richmond and Granville on T1 Western and T5 Cumberland Lines. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
NRL: Eels v Bulldogs at CommBank Stadium in Parramatta. NRL kick-off at 4.05pm, full time around 5.50pm.

With thousands of fans expected, traffic will be heavy on roads approaching Parramatta and nearby car parks are expected to be in high demand.

Allow extra travel time on roads including Parramatta Road, Church Street, Victoria Road, James Ruse Drive and Pennant Hills Road. 

Motorists should also exercise caution as pedestrian activity will be high on roads between Parramatta Interchange and the stadium. 

Drivers are reminded that light rail services are running through Parramatta CBD. Trams can’t stop quickly or easily – so exercise extra caution when driving in the area. 

Road closures 

Roads around the stadium will close from 5.50pm until 6.20pm approx. to allow crowds to safely leave the venue. Roads will re-open once crowds have cleared the area. 

  • O’Connell Street from Grose Street to George Street 
  • Ross Street from O’Connell Street to Trott Street 
  • Victoria Road from O’Connell Street to Villiers Street 

While closures are in place motorists are encouraged to avoid driving through Parramatta CBD and detour around the closures via Grose Street, Brickfield Street, Victoria Road and Wilde Avenue in both directions.

properties.publicTransport: Travel on transport is included in match tickets and additional light rail services will run. L2 & L3 light rail services will not operate from Circular Quay to Central due to maintenance. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
NRL: Roosters v Knights at Allianz Stadium. Kick-off at 2pm, full time at 3.50pm.

Traffic delays are expected on roads approaching Moore Park as fans make their way to the precinct

Motorists should carpool and allow plenty of extra travel time on roads including the Eastern Distributor, Moore Park Rd, Cleveland St, Anzac Pde and Lang Rd.

Changed traffic conditions:

  • Driver Avenue will be partially closed outside the stadium from 11am and will reopen once crowds have safely cleared the area.   
  • No through-traffic will be available on Driver Avenue, however access to EP3 grass car park and drop-off areas will be maintained from Lang Road. Grass car park open from 11am.   
  • Special event clearways will be in place on roads in Moore Park and surrounding areas from 11am to 6pm on sections of Albion Street, Lang Road and Fitzroy Street, and from 6am to 7pm on sections of Cleveland Street

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types, and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

arrangementElements.html:

From 6am to 7pm 

  • Cleveland Street between South Dowling Street and City Road (southern side) 
  • Cleveland Street between Abercrombie Street and South Dowling Street (northern side) 

From 11am to 6pm 

  • Albion Street between Bourke Street and Flinders Street (both sides) 
  • Lang Road between Anzac Parade and Errol Flynn Boulevard (northern side)
  • Fitzroy Street between South Dowling Street and Bourke Street (northern side)

properties.otherAdvice:
Autumn Carnival: Championships Day 2 at Royal Randwick Racecourse. First race at 11.45am, last race from 5.10pm.

Traffic may be heavier than usual on Anzac Pde, Doncaster Ave, High St, Alison Rd and Darley Rd as crowds travel to and from the event.

Motorists should allow extra travel time and be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads.

Special event clearways will be in place at the following times and locations: 

  • 4am – 7pm: Darley Road between Alison Road and the Grand Drive roundabout (western side) 
  • 8am – 7pm: Ascot Street between Doncaster Avenue and the roundabout at the entry to Randwick Racecourse (both sides) 
  • 3pm – 7pm: High Street between Gate Two Avenue and Anzac Parade (southern side) 

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

roads.mainStreet: Anzac Parade, Alison Road, High Street

properties.publicTransport: Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip by public transport.
properties.otherAdvice:
Runway Run/Walk at Western Sydney International Airport.

The Runway Run/Walk event gives runners and walkers the unique opportunity to take part in various distance events on the airport runway before it welcomes its first passengers and planes later this year. Runs start from 7am, with the community walk finishing by 4.30pm.

With 10,000 runners and 10,000 walkers expected, attendees are urged to plan their travel ahead of time, and motorists should expect delays on roads approaching the precinct.

Parking onsite is available to participants who have reserved a spot in advance only.

properties.publicTransport: Travel on transport is included in match tickets and additional light rail and shuttle bus services will run. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
Double header at Moore Park:

  • Super Rugby: Waratahs v Pasifika at Allianz Stadium. Kick-off at 7.35pm, full time at 9.20pm.
  • AFL: Sydney Swans v GWS Giants at the SCG. VFL first bounce at 3.35pm, AFL first bounce at 7.50pm and full time at 10.30pm

Moore Park will be very busy on Friday night with 50,000 AFL and Super Rugby fans travelling to the precinct for matches at neighbouring stadiums kicking off around the same time. Traffic delays are expected on roads approaching Moore Park as rugby fans make their way to the precinct in the already busy evening peak.

Motorists should carpool and allow plenty of extra travel time on roads including the Eastern Distributor, Moore Park Rd, Cleveland St, Anzac Pde and Lang Rd.

Changed traffic conditions:

  • Driver Avenue will be partially closed outside the stadiums from 3.30pm and will reopen once crowds have safely cleared the area.   
  • No through-traffic will be available on Driver Avenue, however access to EP2 Kippax car park will be maintained via Moore Park Road and access to EP3 grass car park and drop-off areas maintained from Lang Road. Car Parks open from 4.45pm. 
  • Special event clearways will be in place on roads in Moore Park and surrounding areas from 3pm to midnight on sections of Albion Street, Chalmers St, Moore Park Road and Lang Road, and from 6am to midnight on sections of Cleveland Street and Fitzroy Street. 

 Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types, and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

  • Cleveland Street between South Dowling Street and City Road (southern side)

  • Chalmers Street between Cleveland Street and Devonshire Street (Western side)

  • Moore Park Road between Flinders Street and Greens Road (northern side) 
  • Moore Park Road between Cook Road and Poate Road (southern side) 
  • Lang Road between Anzac Parade and Errol Flynn Boulevard (northern side)

.A-League Men: Central Coast Mariners v Brisbane Roar at polytec Stadium in Gosford. Kick off at 7.35pm, full time approximately 9.25pm

Traffic will be heavier than usual on roads approaching the stadium as fans travel to and from the match. Allow extra travel time and use local parking. 

Dane Drive will be closed from 6pm between the Central Coast Highway and Donnison Street and will reopen once crowds have cleared the area

properties.publicTransport: Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
A Day to Remember & Papa Roach performs at Qudos Bank Arena. Show runs from 8pm to 10.30pm.

  • Traffic delays are expected as concertgoers make their way to Olympic Park during weeknight peak.
  • Motorists should carpool and allow extra travel time on roads including the M4 Mwy, Homebush Bay Dr, Concord Rd, Lane Cove Rd, Parramatta Rd, Centenary Dr and Silverwater Rd.
  • ** PARKING UDPATE ** Pre-booked parking is SOLD OUT for Fri 10 April - don't drive if you haven't pre-booked, catch transport instead.
  • If you have pre-booked your parking, follow the directions of staff and have your parking ticket ready to show at the car park entry.

Road closures are in place in Sydney Olympic Park for the Easter Show. Check out the Sydney Olympic Park Authority interactive map for more details. For detailed closure and access changes in the precinct see the Olympic Park Community page.

arrangementElements.html:
Road closures and changes to parklands will be in place within the Sydney Olympic Park Precinct before, during and after the Easter Show for event set up and pack down. 

Check out the Sydney Olympic Park Authority interactive map for more details. For detailed closure and access changes in the precinct see the Olympic Park Community page. 

Monday 9 March at 1.30am to Tuesday 21 April at 1.30am:   

  • Olympic Boulevard 100m north of Dawn Fraser Avenue adjacent to Cathy Freeman Park amenities, to 200m south of Edwin Flack Avenue outside Qudos Bank Arena.  

Thursday 19 March at 1.30am to Tuesday 21 April at 1.30am:   

  • Australia Avenue from Murray Rose Avenue to Kevin Coombs Avenue. 
  • Showground Road from Murray Rose Avenue to Grand Parade. 
  • Grand Parade from Olympic Boulevard to Australia Avenue.  

Thursday 2 April at 1.30am to Tuesday 14 April at 4am:   

  • Kevin Coombs Avenue from Australia Avenue to Pondage Link.  
  • Marjorie Jackson Parkway from Wentworth Common Carpark to Australia Avenue.  
  • No right turn available onto Holker Busway from Marjorie Jackson Parkway.  
  • No left turn available from Holker Busway onto Marjorie Jackson Parkway.  
  • Olympic Boulevard from Kevin Coombs Avenue to Sarah Durack Avenue.  
  • Showground Road from Murray Rose Avenue to Dawn Fraser Avenue.  
  • Murray Rose Avenue from Showground Road to Gate 15 and Park Street.  
  • Dawn Fraser Avenue from Edwin Flack Avenue to Showground Road (accessible drop-off/pick-up and Show drop-off/pick-up – Edwin Flack Avenue to Fig Grove).  
  • Dawn Fraser Avenue from Showground Road to Park Street.  
  • Dawn Fraser Avenue from Park Street to Australia Avenue (eastbound only).  
  • Park Street from Dawn Fraser Avenue to Herb Elliott Avenue (northbound only).  
  • Murray Rose Avenue from Park Street to the west side of the entrance or carpark P6D.  
  • Australia Avenue from Dawn Fraser Avenue to Murray Rose Avenue.  
  • Shirley Strickland Avenue from Rod Laver Drive to Olympic Boulevard.  
  • Shirley Strickland Avenue from Olympic Boulevard to the M4 Exit (P4 car park).  
  • Rod Laver Drive from Shirley Strickland Avenue to the Tennis Centre.  
  • Shane Gould Avenue at P2A Car Park. (eastern direction).

properties.otherAdvice:
A-League Men Newcastle Jets v Adelaide United at McDonald Jones Stadium. Kick-off at 5pm. Full time at approx. 7pm.

Traffic may be heavier than usual as fans travel to and from the match. Allow extra travel time on Turton Rd, Griffiths Rd, Newcastle Rd, Lambton Rd and Bridges Rd through Broadmeadow while fans are making their way to and from the stadium.

For passenger drop-off, use the allocated 10-minute loading zone in the McDonald Jones Stadium car park. Entry is via the driveway off Turton Road.

Motorists should allow extra travel time and be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads.﻿

webLinks.linkText: Sydney Olympic Park Authority - Community Impacts Easter Show 2025

properties.publicTransport: Travel on public transport is included in tickets to the Show. Express trains and Major Event Buses will run daily. Regular route buses 525, 526, 533 and N81 in Olympic Park will use different stops while roads are closed. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
Hundreds of thousands of people are expected to visit the annual Sydney Royal Easter Show over 12 days at Sydney Olympic Park from Thursday 2 to Monday 13 April. 

  • Motorists should allow extra travel time on roads approaching the precinct including the M4 Mwy, Homebush Bay Dr, Concord Rd, Lane Cove Rd, Parramatta Rd, Centenary Dr and Silverwater Rd throughout the show. 
  • If you need to drive, it’s essential to pre-book parking online in advance, available until 11:59pm the night before your visit, unless sold out prior. Note that P5 and P6 car parks are closed during the show so available spaces are limited.
  • * PARKING UPDATE * All available parking spaces are SOLD OUT for Fri 10 Apr & Sat 11 Apr with spaces selling fast for remaining days of the show. If you haven't pre-booked do not drive, you'll need to catch transport.
  • If you have pre-booked parking, follow directions of staff and have your parking ticket ready to show at the car park entry.
  • Consider parking at a nearby commuter car park and continuing your trip by train or bus to Olympic Park, at no extra cost.  

Road closures and changes to parklands are in place within the Sydney Olympic Park Precinct from Monday 9 March until Tuesday 21 April before, during and after the Easter Show including sections of Dawn Fraser Ave, Olympic Blvd, Showground Rd, Murray Rose Ave, Kevin Coombs Ave, Marjorie Jackson Dwy, Shirley Strickland Ave, Rod Laver Dr. Check out the Sydney Olympic Park Authority interactive map for more details. For detailed closure and access changes in the precinct see the Olympic Park Community page.

properties.publicTransport: Some buses will divert while road closures are in place. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
The Greek Orthodox Easter Procession at Rookwood Cemetery will be held on Friday 10 April at 12pm-3pm.

Road closures will be in place on Weekes Avenue from its intersection with Haslem Drive up to Farrar Avenue between 12pm and 3pm to safely facilitate the event.

Vehicles will be detoured via local surrounding streets. Motorists should follow the direction of traffic control on site and be aware of increased pedestrian activity in the area.
